title: 英语易混词与短语动词

items.title: affect vs effect
items.content_full:
affect（动词）= 影响、使产生变化
  • The weather affects my mood.
  • How will this affect the outcome?

effect（名词）= 效果、影响、结果
  • The medicine had a strong effect.
  • The new policy is now in effect.

effect（动词，较正式）= 实现、引起
  • We need to effect change.

记忆关键：affect = Action（动词，A开头），effect = End result（名词，E = End）
items.mnemonic: RAVEN: Remember, Affect = Verb, Effect = Noun
hints: 哪个是动词？想一想 A for Action
大多数情况：a____ 是动词，e____ 是名词

items.title: principal vs principle
items.content_full:
principal（名词）= 校长、负责人；本金
  • The principal of the school.
  • principal and interest（本金和利息）

principal（形容词）= 主要的、最重要的
  • The principal reason.

principle（仅名词）= 原则、原理、准则
  • A matter of principle.
  • the basic principles of physics.

记忆关键：principal 结尾是 pal（朋友）→ 校长是你的 pal；principle 结尾是 ple → 和 rule 一样是规则
items.mnemonic: The principal is your pal. A principle is a rule.
hints: 结尾是 -ple 的是原则，那 -pal 呢？

items.title: their / there / they're
items.content_full:
their（物主代词）= 他们的
  • Their house is big.
  • I like their ideas.

there（副词）= 那里、那边
  • Put it over there.
  • There is a problem.（存在句：There be...）

they're = they are 的缩写
  • They're coming at 6.

替换测试：
- 能替换成 "they are" → they're
- 指地点或存在句 → there
- 表示所属 → their
items.mnemonic: tHERE — HERE is in tHERE (地点); tHEIR — HEIR has possessions (所属)
hints: 试试把它替换成 'they are'，通顺吗？
指地点的是哪个？里面有 'here' 这个词

items.title: lie vs lay（最常混淆）
items.content_full:
lie（不及物）= 躺、位于、在于（过去式 lay，过去分词 lain）
  • I lie on the bed. → I lay on the bed yesterday. → I have lain here for hours.

lay（及物）= 放置、下蛋（过去式 laid，过去分词 laid）
  • Lay the book on the table. → I laid the book there. → I have laid the book down.

记忆关键：lie 不需要宾语（自己躺），lay 需要宾语（放某物）。

lie（说谎）又是另一个词：过去式 lied，过去分词 lied。
items.mnemonic: Lay (put) takes an object — Lay something down. Lie (recline) doesn't — Just lie down.
hints: 需要宾语（放什么）的是 lay，不需要宾语的是 lie
躺下的过去式是什么？想一下 'Yesterday I ____ on the bed'

items.title: Phrasal Verbs: PUT（短语动词）
items.content_full:
put off = 推迟、拖延
  • Don't put off your homework.

put up with = 忍受、容忍
  • I can't put up with the noise.

put out = 熄灭；使不方便
  • Put out the fire. / I hope I'm not putting you out.

put through = 接通（电话）；使经历
  • Let me put you through to the manager.

put forward = 提出（建议）
  • She put forward a new proposal.

put away = 收好、放回原位
  • Put away your toys.
items.mnemonic: off=推开→推迟，up with=举着→忍受，out=火灭了，through=电话接通
hints: put up with 是接受还是忍受？

items.title: Phrasal Verbs: GET（短语动词）
items.content_full:
get along (with) = 相处融洽
  • Do you get along with your coworkers?

get over = 克服、从...恢复
  • It took months to get over the illness.

get through = 完成、度过；接通电话
  • I got through all the work. / I can't get through to him.

get by = 勉强过活、凑合
  • We don't have much money, but we get by.

get away with = 逃脱惩罚
  • He got away with cheating.

get around to = 终于抽出时间做
  • I'll get around to it eventually.
items.mnemonic: along=相处，over=翻过障碍，through=穿过去，by=凑合过，away with=溜了
hints: 克服困难用 get 怎么说？
get by 和 get through 的区别？

items.title: compliment vs complement
items.content_full:
compliment（名词/动词）= 称赞、赞美
  • She gave me a nice compliment.
  • He complimented her on the presentation.

complement（名词/动词）= 补充、使完善
  • The wine is a perfect complement to the cheese.
  • The two skills complement each other.

记忆关键：complIment = I like（我喜欢→赞美）；complement = complete（完善、补充）
items.mnemonic: I like a compl**i**ment. Things that compl**e**ment compl**e**te each other.
hints: 哪个和 'complete'（完善）相关？注意中间的字母

items.title: fewer vs less
items.content_full:
fewer = 用于可数名词（countable nouns）
  • fewer people, fewer cars, fewer mistakes

less = 用于不可数名词（uncountable nouns）
  • less water, less money, less time

例外（实际用法）：
- 时间、金钱、距离、重量等计量单位通常用 less：less than $20, less than 5 miles
- 超市 '10 items or less' 很常见（虽然语法上应该是 fewer）

实用规则：能数清楚的用 fewer，数不清的用 less。不确定时，less 更通用。
hints: 如果能数出具体数量（1,2,3...），用哪个？
对于钱和时间，习惯上用 less 还是 fewer？
